summary: O'Neil expresses deep concern about Florida's COVID-19 trajectory in July 2020, predicting daily death counts would soon reach 600. Drawing on both personal connection to Florida and data analysis, she argues the state is particularly vulnerable to the pandemic—in some ways more so than Manhattan. The post serves as a teaser for her Bloomberg column where she explains her methodology for this grim prediction.
key_points: Florida's vulnerability to COVID-19 may exceed Manhattan's despite different demographics
Data analysis suggests daily death counts could reach 600 in the coming weeks
Personal connection to Florida through family history motivates her concern
The prediction is based on 'data and some reckoning'—quantitative analysis combined with interpretation
money_quotes: It's a really vulnerable place, in some ways more so than Manhattan.
According to the data, and some reckoning, I figure the daily death counts will soon hit 600.
key_insight: Data analysis in July 2020 suggested Florida's COVID death toll trajectory could rival or exceed New York's catastrophic early pandemic numbers.
